Records.authors: Liu, Yuanche, Matijašić, Antonela, Miczajka, Julian, Xu, Yingxuan, Xu, Yongqun, Zhang, Yang
Records.description: First analytic computation of a three-loop five-point Feynman integral family in dimensional regularization. The pentagon-box-box (PBB) family has 11 propagators, 7 irreducible scalar products ($D_{12}$-$D_{18}$), 316 master integrals, and is evaluated up to transcendental weight 6 ($\varepsilon^6$) via canonical differential equations. The symbol alphabet consists of the 31 planar pentagon letters from Chicherin-Henn (the non-planar letters $W_{20+i}$ are absent). Up to weight 3, results are expressed in terms of classical polylogarithms ($\log$, $\operatorname{Li}_2$, $\operatorname{Li}_3$). A single parity-odd weight-3 function $P$ requires 526 terms plus beyond-the-symbol corrections. Weights 4-6 are given as one-fold integrals over products of weight-3 functions with weight-2 kernels, using a novel auxiliary matrix $\tilde{B}$ satisfying $d\tilde{B} = (dA~)\tilde{A}$. Boundary values are determined analytically from spurious pole cancellation at the symmetric Euclidean point $(-1,-1,-1,-1,-1)$. IBP reduction uses computational algebraic geometry via NeatIBP (~85000 identities). Numerical validation against pySecDec and AMFlow is provided. Ancillary files at https://github.com/YongqunXu/3L5P_PBB.
